# Navigating Cancer Treatment: Insights into the Role of Personalized Care Plans

Categories: 

 [patient outcomes](/blogs/blog/tagged/patient-outcomes)
, 

 [Personalized Care](/blogs/blog/tagged/personalized-care)
, 

 [tailored approaches](/blogs/blog/tagged/tailored-approaches)
, 

[Cancer Treatment](/blogs/blog/tagged/cancer-treatment)

## Understanding the Importance of Personalized Care Plans in Cancer Treatment

Embarking on a cancer treatment journey can be daunting, but understanding the role of personalized care plans can make a significant difference in patient outcomes. These plans are not just a set of instructions; they are a comprehensive approach tailored to meet the unique needs of each patient. By focusing on your specific medical history, current health status, and personal preferences, personalized care plans ensure that every aspect of your treatment is optimized for the best possible results.

In the realm of cancer treatment, personalized care plans are more than a luxury—they are a necessity. They offer a structured yet flexible approach that adapts to the evolving needs of patients, providing a roadmap that guides them through the complexities of their treatment journey. This approach aligns perfectly with the values of those who prioritize quality of life and evidence-based treatments, ensuring that every decision made is in the best interest of the patient.

### The Role of Personalized Care Plans in Enhancing Patient Outcomes

Personalized care plans have a profound impact on patient outcomes, offering a more nuanced and effective approach to cancer treatment. By considering the individual characteristics of each patient, these plans allow healthcare providers to tailor treatments that are not only more effective but also less taxing on the body. This personalized approach can lead to improved patient outcomes, as treatments are specifically designed to work in harmony with the patient's unique biological makeup.

Furthermore, personalized care plans facilitate better communication between patients and healthcare providers. By involving you in the decision-making process, these plans empower you to take an active role in your treatment, fostering a sense of control and confidence. This collaborative approach is crucial in building a trusting relationship between you and your healthcare team, ultimately contributing to better adherence to treatment protocols and improved health outcomes.

### Tailored Approaches for Diverse Cancer Types

The diversity of cancer types necessitates a tailored approach to treatment, and personalized care plans are at the forefront of this strategy. Whether you are dealing with a common cancer type or a rare variant, personalized care plans ensure that your treatment is specifically designed to target the unique characteristics of your cancer. This not only enhances the effectiveness of the treatment but also minimizes potential side effects, allowing you to maintain a better quality of life during your recovery.

For example, patients with breast cancer may benefit from a different treatment regimen than those with lung cancer, even if both are receiving chemotherapy. Personalized care plans take into account the specific genetic markers and molecular profiles of your cancer, allowing for a more targeted and effective treatment. This level of customization is crucial in achieving the best possible outcomes, as it ensures that every aspect of the treatment is aligned with your specific needs and circumstances.

### Integrating Advanced Technologies into Personalized Care Plans

Incorporating advanced technologies into personalized care plans is a game-changer in the field of cancer treatment. From genomic testing to advanced imaging techniques, these technologies provide valuable insights that inform the development of personalized care plans. By leveraging these tools, healthcare providers can gain a deeper understanding of your cancer, leading to more precise and effective treatment strategies.

Genomic testing, for instance, allows for the identification of specific genetic mutations that may be driving the growth of your cancer. This information can be used to tailor treatments that target these mutations, increasing the likelihood of a successful outcome. Similarly, advanced imaging techniques provide detailed visualizations of your tumor, enabling healthcare providers to monitor its response to treatment and make necessary adjustments to your personalized care plan.

By integrating these advanced technologies into your personalized care plan, healthcare providers can offer a more comprehensive and effective approach to cancer treatment. This not only enhances the precision of the treatment but also provides you with the reassurance that every possible measure is being taken to ensure your recovery.

### The Significance of Patient-Centered Communication

Effective communication is a cornerstone of personalized care plans in cancer treatment. When you are well-informed about your treatment options and involved in decision-making, it enhances your confidence and engagement in the process. This patient-centered communication ensures that you fully understand the implications of each treatment choice, allowing you to make informed decisions that align with your values and preferences.

Healthcare providers at South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac are committed to maintaining open lines of communication. They prioritize listening to your concerns, answering your questions, and providing clear explanations about your treatment plan. This approach not only builds trust but also empowers you to take an active role in your care, ultimately contributing to better patient outcomes.

### Addressing Emotional and Psychological Needs

Cancer treatment is not just a physical journey; it also involves emotional and psychological challenges. Personalized care plans acknowledge the importance of addressing these aspects to ensure comprehensive care. By integrating support services such as counseling and support groups, these plans help you navigate the emotional complexities of cancer treatment.

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, the focus is on providing a holistic approach that considers your overall well-being. This includes offering resources that help you manage stress, anxiety, and depression, which are common during cancer treatment. By addressing these needs, personalized care plans contribute to a more positive treatment experience and improve your quality of life.

### Optimizing Treatment with Onsite Laboratories

The availability of onsite laboratories is a significant advantage in the development and execution of personalized care plans. These labs provide immediate access to critical health insights, allowing for timely adjustments to your treatment plan. This real-time data is crucial for monitoring your response to treatment and ensuring that any necessary changes are made promptly.

For patients at South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, the onsite labs facilitate a seamless integration of diagnostic and treatment services. This not only enhances the efficiency of the treatment process but also provides you with the reassurance that your care is being managed with precision and attention to detail. The ability to quickly obtain lab results and adjust your care plan accordingly is a key component of personalized cancer treatment.

### Creating a Comfortable Treatment Environment

The physical environment in which you receive treatment plays a vital role in your overall experience. Personalized care plans often include considerations for creating a comfortable and supportive setting that enhances your well-being. At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, the state-of-the-art infusion suites are designed to provide a calming atmosphere during chemotherapy sessions.

These suites are equipped with comfortable seating, entertainment options, and amenities that cater to your comfort and relaxation. By prioritizing your comfort, personalized care plans help reduce the stress and anxiety often associated with cancer treatment. This focus on creating a positive environment is an integral part of the holistic approach to cancer care.

### Empowering Patients Through Education

Education is a powerful tool in empowering you to take charge of your cancer treatment journey. Personalized care plans often include educational resources that provide you with the knowledge needed to understand your condition and treatment options. This education enables you to actively participate in your care and make informed decisions that align with your goals.

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, patient education is a priority. The team provides you with comprehensive information about your diagnosis, treatment options, and potential side effects. This knowledge empowers you to ask questions, express your preferences, and collaborate with your healthcare team to develop a personalized care plan that meets your needs.

### The Future of Personalized Cancer Treatment

As advancements in medical research and technology continue to evolve, the future of personalized cancer treatment holds great promise. Emerging therapies and innovations in precision medicine are paving the way for even more tailored approaches to cancer care. By staying at the forefront of these developments, South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac is committed to providing you with the most advanced and effective treatment options available.

The integration of cutting-edge technologies and personalized care plans ensures that you receive a treatment approach that is not only effective but also aligned with your unique needs and preferences. As the field of cancer treatment continues to advance, personalized care plans will remain a crucial component in optimizing patient outcomes and enhancing the overall treatment experience.

### Advancements in Personalized Medicine

The landscape of cancer treatment is rapidly evolving with the advent of personalized medicine. This approach leverages genetic information to tailor cancer treatments specifically to your DNA. By understanding the genetic mutations that drive your cancer, healthcare providers can develop targeted therapies that are more effective and have fewer side effects. Personalized medicine represents a significant shift from the traditional one-size-fits-all model, offering a more precise and individualized approach to cancer care.

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, the integration of personalized medicine into care plans is a testament to their commitment to providing cutting-edge treatment options. By utilizing the latest advancements in genomic testing and molecular profiling, the clinic ensures that your treatment plan is as unique as your genetic makeup. This focus on personalized medicine enhances the effectiveness of cancer treatment, improving your chances of a successful outcome.

### Collaborative Care Teams

The complexity of cancer treatment often requires the expertise of a multidisciplinary team. Personalized care plans facilitate collaboration among various specialists, ensuring a comprehensive approach to your treatment. Oncologists, hematologists, radiologists, and support staff work together to develop a care plan that addresses all aspects of your health and well-being.

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, the collaborative care team is dedicated to providing you with the highest standard of care. By fostering open communication and collaboration among team members, the clinic ensures that all facets of your treatment are seamlessly integrated. This team-based approach not only enhances the quality of care but also provides you with the support and reassurance you need during your cancer journey.

### Enhancing Quality of Life Through Supportive Care

Supportive care is an integral component of personalized care plans, focusing on improving your quality of life during cancer treatment. This includes managing symptoms, addressing side effects, and providing emotional and psychological support. By prioritizing your comfort and well-being, supportive care ensures that you can maintain a fulfilling life while undergoing treatment.

South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac is committed to offering comprehensive supportive care services. From pain management to nutritional counseling, the clinic provides a range of resources designed to enhance your quality of life. By addressing your physical, emotional, and social needs, supportive care plays a crucial role in your overall treatment plan.

### Patient Empowerment Through Shared Decision-Making

Shared decision-making is a fundamental aspect of personalized care plans, empowering you to take an active role in your treatment. By involving you in the decision-making process, healthcare providers ensure that your preferences and values are considered in every aspect of your care. This collaborative approach fosters a sense of ownership and engagement, contributing to better patient outcomes.

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, shared decision-making is at the core of the patient experience. The clinic values your input and strives to provide you with the information and support needed to make informed decisions about your treatment. By fostering a partnership between you and your healthcare team, shared decision-making enhances the effectiveness of personalized care plans.

### Emphasizing Preventive Care and Early Detection

Preventive care and early detection are key components of personalized care plans, enabling healthcare providers to identify and address potential health issues before they become significant problems. Regular screenings and monitoring allow for the early detection of cancer, increasing the likelihood of successful treatment and improving patient outcomes.

South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac emphasizes the importance of preventive care and early detection in their personalized care plans. By offering comprehensive screening services and utilizing advanced diagnostic tools, the clinic ensures that you receive the best possible care. This proactive approach is essential in maintaining your health and well-being throughout your cancer journey.

### Contact Us for Personalized Cancer Care

At South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac, we understand that cancer treatment is a deeply personal journey. Our commitment to personalized care plans ensures that you receive the support and treatment tailored to your unique needs. Our team of experienced oncologists and healthcare professionals is dedicated to providing you with compassionate, evidence-based care that prioritizes your health and well-being.

If you are seeking personalized cancer treatment that aligns with your values and preferences, we invite you to reach out to us. Contact us at [jessie.hicks@usoncology.com](mailto:jessie.hicks@usoncology.com) to learn more about how we can support you on your journey to recovery. # The Role of Personalized Oncology Care in Enhancing Patient Experience

Categories: 

 [Oncology](/blogs/blog/tagged/oncology)
, 

 [Patient Care](/blogs/blog/tagged/patient-care)
, 

[Cancer Treatment](/blogs/blog/tagged/cancer-treatment)

## Introduction

In the evolving landscape of cancer treatment, personalized oncology care has emerged as a pivotal approach in enhancing the patient experience. As medical advancements continue to redefine treatment paradigms, the focus on individualized care has become more pronounced, offering patients not just hope but also a tailored journey towards recovery. This blog explores the vital role that personalized oncology care plays in improving patient outcomes and experiences, particularly within the context of South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac.

## Understanding Personalized Oncology Care

Personalized oncology care is a patient-centered approach that tailors treatment plans to the unique genetic, environmental, and lifestyle factors of each individual. Unlike traditional methods that often apply a one-size-fits-all strategy, personalized care delves into the specifics of a patient’s biology and personal circumstances. This approach not only optimizes treatment efficacy but also minimizes potential side effects, significantly impacting the overall patient experience.

At South Florida Oncology, the integration of advanced treatment options with personalized care models ensures that patients receive the most effective therapies tailored to their specific needs. This is achieved through comprehensive genetic testing, detailed patient assessments, and continuous monitoring of treatment responses.

## The Importance of Patient Experience in Cancer Care

Patient experience is a critical component of healthcare delivery, particularly in oncology, where the emotional and physical demands are profound. A positive patient experience not only fosters trust and satisfaction but also contributes to better health outcomes. Personalized oncology care enhances the patient experience by ensuring that each individual feels heard, understood, and actively involved in their treatment journey.

For patients at South Florida Oncology, this means receiving care in a supportive environment where their preferences and concerns are prioritized. The clinic's commitment to compassionate care is reflected in its state-of-the-art facilities, including comfortable infusion suites and on-site laboratories, which streamline the treatment process and enhance patient convenience.

## Advanced Treatment Options: A Pillar of Personalized Care

Advanced treatment options are integral to personalized oncology care, offering patients access to the latest medical innovations and therapies. At South Florida Oncology, patients benefit from cutting-edge treatments that are continuously updated to reflect the latest research and clinical guidelines. This includes targeted therapies, immunotherapy, and precision medicine, which focus on treating cancer at the molecular level.

The availability of these advanced treatment options ensures that patients receive the most effective care possible, tailored to the specific characteristics of their cancer. This not only improves treatment outcomes but also reduces the likelihood of adverse effects, further enhancing the patient experience.

## Personalized Care and Patient Empowerment

Empowerment is a key outcome of personalized oncology care, as it encourages patients to take an active role in their treatment decisions. By providing clear, accessible information and fostering open communication, healthcare providers at South Florida Oncology help patients understand their options and make informed choices about their care.

This empowerment is further supported by the clinic's emphasis on one-on-one consultations, where patients can discuss their concerns and preferences with their oncologists. Such interactions not only build trust but also ensure that treatment plans are aligned with the patient’s values and goals, enhancing their overall experience and satisfaction.

To learn more about how personalized oncology care can enhance your cancer treatment experience, contact us at [jessie.hicks@usoncology.com](mailto:jessie.hicks@usoncology.com). Our team at South Florida Oncology is dedicated to supporting your journey with compassion and expertise.

## Integrating Technology in Personalized Oncology Care

Technology plays a crucial role in the evolution of personalized oncology care, enabling healthcare providers to offer more precise and effective treatments. At South Florida Oncology, cutting-edge technology is seamlessly integrated into the care process, enhancing both diagnostic accuracy and treatment efficacy.

Advanced imaging techniques, such as PET and MRI scans, allow for detailed visualization of tumors, aiding in accurate diagnosis and treatment planning. Additionally, the use of electronic health records (EHRs) ensures that patient information is readily accessible, facilitating coordinated care among the medical team. This technological integration not only streamlines the treatment process but also enhances the overall patient experience by reducing wait times and improving communication.

## The Role of Oncologists in Personalized Care

Oncologists are at the forefront of personalized oncology care, guiding patients through their treatment journey with expertise and empathy. At South Florida Oncology, the team of board-certified oncologists, including Dr. Andrew M. Schneider and Dr. Chintan C. Gandhi, exemplify the commitment to personalized care.

These skilled professionals leverage their extensive knowledge and experience to develop individualized treatment plans that address the unique needs of each patient. By fostering open communication and providing clear explanations of treatment options, oncologists empower patients to make informed decisions about their care. This collaborative approach not only enhances patient satisfaction but also improves treatment adherence and outcomes.

## Patient-Centric Facilities: Enhancing Comfort and Convenience

The physical environment in which cancer treatment is delivered can significantly impact the patient experience. South Florida Oncology prioritizes patient comfort and convenience through its state-of-the-art facilities in Tamarac, Florida.

The clinic's infusion suites are designed with patient comfort in mind, offering a soothing atmosphere for chemotherapy treatments. On-site laboratories provide quick and accurate test results, reducing the need for additional appointments and allowing for timely adjustments to treatment plans. By creating a welcoming and efficient environment, South Florida Oncology ensures that patients feel supported and at ease throughout their treatment journey.

For more information on how South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac can support you with personalized oncology care, please reach out to us at [jessie.hicks@usoncology.com](mailto:jessie.hicks@usoncology.com). Our dedicated team is here to guide you every step of the way, ensuring a compassionate and effective treatment experience.

## Holistic Support: Beyond Medical Treatment

Personalized oncology care extends beyond the medical treatment of cancer to encompass the holistic needs of patients. At South Florida Oncology, this approach is evident in the comprehensive support services offered to patients and their families. From nutritional counseling to psychological support, the clinic ensures that every aspect of a patient's well-being is addressed.

Patients benefit from access to support groups and counseling services that provide emotional and psychological assistance during their cancer journey. These resources are crucial in helping patients cope with the stress and challenges of treatment, fostering resilience and a positive outlook.

## Community Engagement and Education

Community engagement and education are essential components of personalized oncology care. South Florida Oncology actively participates in community outreach programs to raise awareness about cancer prevention, early detection, and treatment options. By educating the public, the clinic empowers individuals to take proactive steps in their health management.

Workshops and seminars conducted by the clinic offer valuable insights into the latest advancements in cancer care, helping patients and their families stay informed about their options. This commitment to education reinforces the clinic's role as a trusted resource in the community, enhancing patient trust and engagement.

## Aligning with the Ideal Customer Profile

South Florida Oncology and Hematology Consultant, PA - Tamarac is dedicated to serving its ideal customer profile by providing comprehensive, personalized care that meets the unique needs of each patient. The clinic's focus on advanced treatment options, patient-centric facilities, and holistic support aligns with the preferences and values of its target demographic.

By prioritizing quality of life and personalized care, South Florida Oncology ensures that patients receive the highest standard of treatment in a supportive and compassionate environment. This strategic alignment not only enhances patient satisfaction but also fosters better health outcomes, reinforcing the clinic's reputation as a leader in oncology care.

# Personalized medicine may result in better outcomes for cancer patients

![](https://www.oncology-central.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/03/DNA-biomarker-1-800x345.jpg)

**Researchers from the University of California San Diego School of Medicine (CA, US) report that the utilization of individual genetics to develop personalized therapeutic approaches for cancer patients has resulted in improved response to treatment and longer periods of remission, even as early as Phase I clinical trials.**

In a meta-analysis published recently in *JAMA Oncology*, the authors reviewed 346 Phase I clinical trials that involved 13,203 cancer patients. The team found that in precision medicine treatment arms, over 30% of patients responded to treatment as opposed to the 4.9 % that responded to treatment in the nonpersonalized arms. Patients that received personalized treatment also displayed improved progression-free survival, with a median of 5.7 months remission before their diseased worsened compared to 2.95 months for the others in the trials.

Lead author Maria Schwaederlé (UC San Diego Moores Cancer Center) commented: “Our analysis shows that in the era of precision medicine, Phase I clinical trials utilizing personalized therapy with a biomarker-based approach can do more than assessing the toxicity and side effects… These early trials can result in improved outcomes for patients, even among people whose disease is resistant to standard treatments, by selecting patients who will respond best using a personalized approach from the start.”

The study, which involved 58 precision medicine treatment arms and 293 nonpersonalized arms, also demonstrated that personalized medicine led to improved outcomes in different tumor types. Precision medicine was associated with improved treatment response rates in 24.5% of patients with solid tumors compared to a response of 4.5% in patients receiving nonpersonalized treatments; this was echoed in blood cancer patients, with 24.5% responding compared to 13.5% respectively. Across the tumor types, precision medicine led to longer progression-free survival, with 4.1 months compared to 2.8 months in solid tumors and 13.6 months compared to 4 months in blood cancers.

A series of subanalyses of 234 arms demonstrated first, that biomarker-assigned targeted therapies lead to improved response rates (31.1% response rates in personalized arms compared to 5.1% in other patients); and second, that therapies paired to biomarkers targeting genomic alterations performed better than biomarkers directed at protein overexpression, with a 42% response rate compared to 22.4% respectively.

“What we observed is that Phase I trials can serve both to inform us on the effectiveness of new therapies as well as identify patients likely to benefit most if a personalized approach is employed,” stated senior author Razelle Kurzrock (UC San Diego Moores Cancer Center). “Another important point is that targeted drugs in and of themselves are often quite useless if not combined with a patient’s individual tumor biomarkers to determine whether they are likely to benefit from a particular therapy,” Kurzrock concluded.

Source: <https://health.ucsd.edu/news/releases/Pages/2016-06-06-personalized-medicine-leads-to-better-cancer-outcomes.aspx>

# Personalized Cancer Therapy Improves Outcomes in Advanced Disease, Says Study

October 2, 2020 |
Yadira Galindo

​Patients receiving care for advanced cancer at Moores Cancer Center at UC San Diego Health were more likely to survive or experience a longer period without their disease progressing if they received personalized cancer therapy, report University of California San Diego School of Medicine researchers.

Led by Razelle Kurzrock, MD, director of the Center for Personalized Cancer Therapy at Moores Cancer Center and senior author of the study, a multidisciplinary molecular tumor board was established to advise treating physicians on course of care using an individual patient’s molecular tumor makeup to design precision medicine strategies.

"Patients who underwent a molecular tumor board-recommended therapy were better matched to genomic alterations in their cancer and had improved outcomes," said Kurzrock. "The three-year survival for patients with the highest degree of matching and who received a personalized cancer therapy was approximately 55 percent compared to 25 percent in patients who received therapy that was unmatched or had low degrees of matching."

Of 429 patients evaluated by the molecular tumor board, 62 percent were matched to at least one drug, report the researchers in the October 2, 2020 online issue of  *[Nature Communications](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-020-18613-3)*. Twenty percent of patients matched to all recommended drugs, including combination therapies.

The tumor board acted in an advisory role and treating physicians chose not to use the board’s recommended strategy in 38 percent of cases, opting instead for a standard therapy approach that might have been unmatched to the patient’s genetic alterations or had a low degree of matching. These patients experienced a lower progression-free survival and overall survival rates.

The use of next-generation sequencing allows for the identification of novel potential targets for patients with cancer to improve outcomes, but there are challenges to using this approach widely, said [Shumei Kato, MD](https://providers.ucsd.edu/details/32585/medical-oncology-cancer), associate professor of medicine at UC San Diego School of Medicine and first author.

"One of the hurdles is that every cancer patient appears to be carrying different molecular and genomic patterns despite having the same cancer type," said Kato, a Moores Cancer Center medical oncologist specializing in rare and gastrointestinal cancers. "This can be challenging since we are customizing therapy based on the unique genomic pattern patients have, and thus it is difficult to predict the response. In addition, this approach requires multidisciplinary expertise as well as access to drugs or clinical trials not always available in smaller practices."

At Moores Cancer Center, the molecular tumor board is composed of experts in basic, transitional and clinical research as well as bioinformatics, genetics, radiology, pathology and physicians in multiple specialties such as medical, surgical and radiation oncology.

Further clinical investigations with a larger sample size are necessary to identify the matching score thresholds that determine the usefulness of a precision medicine approach, said the researchers.

Co-authors include Amelie Boichard, Mina Nikanjam, Elizabeth Weihe, Dennis J. Kuo, Ramez N. Eskander, Aaron Goodman, Natalie Galanina, Paul T. Fanta, Richard B. Schwab, Rebecca Shatsky, Steven C. Plaxe, Andrew Sharabi, Ryosuke Okamura, Suzanna Lee, Scott M. Lippman, Jason K. Sicklick, all of UC San Diego; Ki Hwan Kim, UC San Diego and Seoul National University; Hyo Jeong Lim, UC San Diego and Veterans Health Service Medical Center in Seoul; Edward Stites, Salk Institute for Biological Studies; and Jacob J. Adashek, University of South Florida.

This research was funded, in part, by the National Institutes of Health (P30 CA023100) and the Joan and Irwin Jacobs Fund.

*Disclosures: Kato serves as a consultant for Foundation Medicine and receives speaker’s fees from Roche. Eskander serves as a consultant for GSK, Merck, Eisai, Clovis Oncology, AstraZenica, Pfizer and Iovance. He has received speaker fees from AstraZenica and Merck. Sharabi reports research funding and honoraria from Pfizer and Varian Medical Systems, consultant fees from AstraZeneca and Jounce Therapeutics, personal fees from Merck, and is the scientific founder with equity interest in Toragen Inc. outside the submitted work. Sicklick receives research funds from Foundation Medicine Inc. and Amgen, as well as consultant fees from Grand Rounds, Deciphera and LOXO. Kurzrock has research funding from Incyte, Genentech, Merck Serono, Pfizer, Sequenom, Foundation Medicine, Guardant Health, Grifols, and Konica Minolta, as well as consultant fees from LOXO, X-Biotech, Actuate Therapeutics, Genentech, Pfizer and NeoMed. She receives speaker fees from Roche, and has an equity interest in IDbyDNA and CureMatch, Inc. She is a co-founder of CureMatch and board member of CureMatch and CureMetrix.*

enriched_points.point_title: Improved Patient Outcomes with Personalized Therapies
enriched_points.expanded_content: Personalized therapies utilize genetic information to tailor treatments, leading to higher response rates and longer progression-free survival, as evidenced by recent clinical studies.
Targeted therapies minimize exposure to ineffective treatments, reducing the incidence and severity of side effects, thereby enhancing the patient's quality of life during treatment.
Personalized care plans foster patient engagement and understanding, leading to improved adherence to treatment protocols and better overall outcomes in cancer management.
